title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Discover how to navigate NZ school holidays with our expert travel tips and save up to 30% on accommodation
intro: If you're planning to travel during NZ school holidays, be prepared for crowds and higher prices. However, with some smart planning, you can save up to 30% on accommodation and activities. In this article, we'll share our top tips for beating the crowds and making the most of your family holiday.

sections.heading: Understanding Peak vs Off-Peak Pricing
sections.body: During NZ school holidays, prices for accommodation, activities, and tourist attractions can skyrocket. For example, a family room at the Novotel Auckland Ellerslie can cost upwards of $450 per night during peak season, compared to $280 per night during off-peak periods. Understanding these pricing differences can help you make informed decisions about when to travel.

sections.heading: Booking Lead Times
list: Book your accommodation at least 6 months in advance to secure the best prices
Use booking platforms like Booking.com or Expedia to compare prices and find deals
Consider booking a package deal that includes accommodation, activities, and car rental for added convenience and savings

sections.heading: Top Tips for Beating the Crowds
sections.body: To avoid the crowds, consider visiting popular attractions like the Sky Tower or Te Puia early in the morning or later in the evening. You can also use the 2-hour drive from Auckland to Hamilton to your advantage by visiting the Hamilton Zoo or Waikato Museum during off-peak hours.

sections.heading: Saving Money on Activities
list: Look for package deals that include multiple activities, such as a Skyline Gondola and Luge combo in Queenstown
Use coupons or vouchers to discount individual activities, such as a $20 discount on a Fullers Ferry ticket to Waiheke Island
Consider purchasing an annual pass to popular attractions, such as the Auckland Zoo or Kelly Tarlton's Sea Life Aquarium, for $150 per person
